content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Find if it is possible to represent NNN as the sum of several(possibly zero) 222's and several(possibly zero) 777's.

Formally, find if there exist two integers X,Y (X,Y≥0)X, Y \ (X, Y \ge 0)X,Y (X,Y≥0) such that 2⋅X+7⋅Y=N2 \cdot X + 7\cdot Y = N2⋅X+7⋅Y=N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing an integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line `YES` if it is possible to represent NNN as the sum of several(possibly zero) 222's and several(possibly zero) 777's and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: 2⋅1+7⋅0=22 \cdot 1 + 7\cdot 0 = 22⋅1+7⋅0=2.
Test case 222: It is impossible to express 555 as a sum of several 222's and 777's.
Test case 333: 2⋅0+7⋅1=72 \cdot 0 + 7\cdot 1 = 72⋅0+7⋅1=7.
Test case 444: 2⋅2+7⋅1=112 \cdot 2 + 7\cdot 1 = 112⋅2+7⋅1=11.
